mnemonics: C-K P-Squared: To find the probability of going from state C to state K in 2 steps, sum all Paths (P) where you Squared (for step 2) the probabilities.
Power of P: The n-step transition probabilities are found by raising the one-step matrix P to the power of n.
Future is Independent: Remember 'Markov' for the property that the future depends only on the present state, not the past. This is the key assumption that makes the Chapman-Kolmogorov equations work.
keyPoints: The (i, j)-th entry of the matrix Pⁿ is the probability of being in state j after n steps, starting from state i.
The Chapman-Kolmogorov equations provide a method to compute n-step probabilities by considering all intermediate states k at a chosen time step.
The initial state distribution (a row vector) is multiplied by the transition matrix Pⁿ to get the state distribution after n steps.
commonMistakes: Misindexing the transition matrix. Remember: the entry P_ij is the probability of going FROM state i TO state j.
Forgetting to sum over all possible intermediate states k when using the Chapman-Kolmogorov equations manually.
Attempting to multiply matrices element-wise instead of using proper matrix multiplication, which leads to incorrect results.
